_meta.note: Post-teardown snapshot reconstructed from documented UAT outcomes. rg install fully torn down. km install verified healthy after ssm-session-doc re-apply.
_meta.teardown_method: km uninit --force + km unbootstrap + manual DynamoDB lock table delete

dynamodb.note: rg DynamoDB lock table (tf-rg-locks-use1) manually deleted after km unbootstrap failed to remove it. km install tables (km-sandboxes, tf-km-locks-use1) unchanged.

efs.note: rg EFS (fs-06dcf5e3b8acc3a27) destroyed by km uninit. km install EFS unchanged.

iam.note: All rg-* IAM roles (rg-create-handler, rg-ttl-handler, rg-email-create-handler, rg-budget-enforcer-*, rg-ec2spot-ssm-*) removed by km uninit. km-* roles unchanged from before snapshot.

kms.note: rg KMS alias (alias/rg-platform) removed. Key itself scheduled for deletion 2026-05-25 (7-day AWS minimum window). km install KMS alias and key unchanged.

lambda.note: rg Lambda functions (rg-create-handler, rg-ttl-handler, rg-email-create-handler) destroyed by km uninit. km-* functions unchanged.

s3.note: rg-artifacts-052251888500 and rg-artifacts-052251888500-replica removed by km unbootstrap. tf-rg-state-use1 removed by km unbootstrap. km install buckets (km-artifacts-12345, km-artifacts-12345-replica) unchanged.

ses.note: After rg teardown: only km-* rules remain in sandbox-email-shared rule set. No rg-* orphan rules present. km install SES domain identity and DKIM records unchanged.

route53.note: Both hosted zones intact. DKIM CNAMEs belong to km install. No rg-specific Route53 changes (rg shared the domain identity with km).

ssm.note: rg SSM parameters (/rg/config/remote-create/safe-phrase, etc.) removed by km uninit. km install SSM parameters unchanged. KM-Sandbox-Session SSM document re-created via terragrunt apply after rg teardown destroyed it (cross-install shared-resource teardown bug — see deferred-items.md).

vpc.note: rg VPC removed by km uninit network module destroy. km install VPC unchanged.

km_install_isolation_verdict.caveat: KM-Sandbox-Session SSM document was deleted by rg teardown (cross-install shared-resource destruction bug). Required manual re-apply of km ssm-session-doc module to restore. After re-apply, km install fully healthy. This is a TIER-1 gap for 84.4.1.
